heading: Employees
label: 7471 Employees
notes: References in TextThe effective date of this subsection, referred to in subsec. (a)(2)(B), (8), probably means the effective date of , which amended subsec. (a) generally. See Effective Date of 2011 Amendment note below.section 1(a) of Pub. L. 111–366

Amendments2011—Subsec. (a). amended subsec. (a) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The Tax Court is authorized to appoint, in accordance with the provisions of title 5, United States Code, governing appointment in the competitive service, and to fix the basic pay of, in accordance with chapter 51 and subchapter III of chapter 53 of such title, such employees as may be necessary efficiently to execute the functions vested in the Tax Court.”Pub. children.heading: Clerk
children.label: 1 Clerk
children.text: The Tax Court may appoint a clerk without regard to the provisions of title 5, United States Code, governing appointments in the competitive service. The clerk shall serve at the pleasure of the Tax Court.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The judges and special trial judges of the Tax Court may appoint employees, in such numbers as the Tax Court may approve, without regard to the provisions of title 5, United States Code, governing appointments in the competitive service. Any such employee shall serve at the pleasure of the appointing judge.

children.heading: Exemption from Federal leave provisions
children.label: B Exemption from Federal leave provisions
children.text: A law clerk appointed under this subsection shall be exempt from the provisions of subchapter I of chapter 63 of title 5, United States Code. Any unused sick leave or annual leave standing to the law clerk’s credit as of the effective date of this subsection shall remain credited to the law clerk and shall be available to the law clerk upon separation from the Federal Government.

children.heading: Other employees
children.label: 3 Other employees
children.text: The Tax Court may appoint necessary employees without regard to the provisions of title 5, United States Code, governing appointments in the competitive service. Such employees shall be subject to removal by the Tax Court.

children.heading: Pay
children.label: 4 Pay
children.text: The Tax Court may fix and adjust the compensation for the clerk and other employees of the Tax Court without regard to the provisions of chapter 51, subchapter III of chapter 53, or , United States Code. To the maximum extent feasible, the Tax Court shall compensate employees at rates consistent with those for employees holding comparable positions in courts established under Article III of the Constitution of the United States.section 5373 of title 5

children.heading: Programs
children.label: 5 Programs
children.text: The Tax Court may establish programs for employee evaluations, incentive awards, flexible work schedules, premium pay, and resolution of employee grievances.

children.label: A
children.text: prohibit discrimination on the basis of race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, political affiliation, marital status, or handicapping condition; and

children.label: B
children.text: promulgate procedures for resolving complaints of discrimination by employees and applicants for employment.

children.heading: Experts and consultants
children.label: 7 Experts and consultants
children.text: The Tax Court may procure the services of experts and consultants under , United States Code.section 3109 of title 5

children.label: A
children.text: appeal a reduction in grade or removal to the Merit Systems Protection Board under chapter 43 of title 5, United States Code,

children.label: B
children.text: appeal an adverse action to the Merit Systems Protection Board under chapter 75 of title 5, United States Code,

children.label: C
children.text: appeal a prohibited personnel practice described under , United States Code, to the Merit Systems Protection Board under chapter 77 of that title,section 2302(b) of title 5

children.label: D
children.text: make an allegation of a prohibited personnel practice described under , United States Code, with the Office of Special Counsel under chapter 12 of that title for action in accordance with that chapter, orsection 2302(b) of title 5

children.label: E
children.text: file an appeal with the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission under part 1614 of title 29 of the Code of Federal Regulations,

children.heading: Competitive status
children.label: 9 Competitive status
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, any employee of the Tax Court who has completed at least 1 year of continuous service under a non-temporary appointment with the Tax Court acquires a competitive status for appointment to any position in the competitive service for which the employee possesses the required qualifications.

children.heading: Merit system principles, prohibited personnel practices, and preference eligibles
children.label: 10 Merit system principles, prohibited personnel practices, and preference eligibles
A
children.text: include the principles set forth in , United States Code;section 2301(b) of title 5

children.label: B
children.text: prohibit personnel practices prohibited under , United States Code; andsection 2302(b) of title 5

children.label: C
children.text: in the case of any individual who would be a preference eligible in the executive branch, provide preference for that individual in a manner and to an extent consistent with preference accorded to preference eligibles in the executive branch.

children.heading: Expenses for travel and subsistence
children.label: b Expenses for travel and subsistence
children.text: The employees of the Tax Court shall receive their necessary traveling expenses, and expenses for subsistence while traveling on duty and away from their designated stations, as provided in chapter 57 of title 5, United States Code.

children.heading: Special trial judges
children.label: c Special trial judges
children.text: For compensation and travel and subsistence allowances of special trial judges of the Tax Court, see subsections (d) and (e) of section 7443A.
